body:
Randy, you mentioned on the phone last week that the Desert Bloom group might have a hard one coming up — something about a hearing for one of the women that kept getting moved. I've been thinking about it since then and just wanted to check in. Did it go the way you hoped?

Also, I've got Nana's tamale pie recipe and was thinking of doing dinner at the house this weekend if you and the kids can manage it. Let me know what the schedule looks like. — Mom
snippet: Randy, you mentioned on the phone last week that the Desert Bloom group might ha...
subject: Desert Bloom — Tuesday's session?

body:
The hearing got continued again — third time — and I had to deliver that news in the group setting, which is not how you want to do it. She took it better than I did, if I'm honest. The others rallied round her straightaway, which is exactly what the group is there for, so I'll call it a win even if the win tastes like cold coffee.

Dinner Sunday works. I'll bring Megan if she's not at Robert's that weekend. Rosa's covering the morning calendar so I should be free by noon — call it one o'clock at yours, two if a judge decides to be theatrical. Either way, save me a corner piece. — R
snippet: The hearing got continued again — third time — and I had to deliver that news in...
subject: Re: Desert Bloom — Tuesday's session?

body:
Randy, haven't seen you in almost three weeks and I'm starting to think you've moved to that coffee shop permanently. Karen and Daniel are coming Sunday and I told Megan to bring the kids around two. Nothing fancy — I'm doing the enchiladas you like.

Let me know if you can make it. I know you've had a heavy stretch.
snippet: Randy, haven't seen you in almost three weeks and I'm starting to think you've m...
subject: Sunday dinner at the house?

body:
I'll be there. Trial wrapped Thursday — jury came back fast, which was either a very good or very bad sign, and it turned out to be neither, just a long week condensed into one afternoon. I need a Sunday that doesn't involve a courtroom or a case file.

I'll do my Sabino loop in the morning so I'll be at yours by one, maybe half past. Tell Karen not to bring the politics and I won't bring mine. Megan texted me — she's coming with both kids, so brace yourself.

Anything you need me to pick up on the way? I'm passing Desert Roasters regardless so I can grab coffee if you're running low.
snippet: I'll be there. Trial wrapped Thursday — jury came back fast, which was either a ...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house?

body:
One-thirty is perfect. Grab the coffee — the dark roast, not whatever you drink. Karen is already bringing politics, Daniel is already sighing about it, so you two will cancel each other out and we'll have a nice time.

Megan said Tyler wants to show you something on his tablet. I have no idea what, I just smiled and nodded. Come hungry.
snippet: One-thirty is perfect. Grab the coffee — the dark roast, not whatever you drink....
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house?

body:
Randy, I'm doing a proper roast Sunday around 5. Was hoping you could bring Megan and Tyler. Karen and Daniel are coming, so the kids will have their cousins to run around with in the backyard. Daniel's been asking about Tyler's soccer situation.

Let me know if Robert has them Sunday or if you need me to sort out a lift for Megan from her thing. And tell me now if Tyler has decided he doesn't eat anything normal this week — I'll adjust.
snippet: Randy, I'm doing a proper roast Sunday around 5. Was hoping you could bring Mega...
subject: Sunday dinner at the house - you and the kids?

body:
Sunday works. Robert has them Saturday night but I pick up at noon, so 5 is fine. I'll bring them both. Tyler's soccer is done for the season — he'll be thrilled to see the cousins and will probably eat half your kitchen regardless of what's in it. Megan might need a ride from her friend's in Marana around 3, I'll sort that.

It's been a rough week in court. A roast sounds exactly right. Don't go to any trouble on my account but please do anyway.
snippet: Sunday works. Robert has them Saturday night but I pick up at noon, so 5 is fine...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house - you and the kids?

body:
Good, I'll plan for six of you plus the cousins. I'll do a lamb shoulder — enough for everyone and leftovers for your fridge because I know what your week looks like.

Karen mentioned you've got a big case on. She didn't say more than that. I won't ask. Just come hungry and leave the rest at the door for one evening. Pick up Megan, I'll keep everything warm if you're a bit late.
snippet: Good, I'll plan for six of you plus the cousins. I'll do a lamb shoulder — enoug...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house - you and the kids?

body:
Lamb shoulder. You are a good woman, Mum.

I've got Rosa covering my Monday morning handoff so I can stay a bit longer Sunday if it stretches. I'll bring a decent red — nothing fancy, just something that goes with the fact that it's been a long week and Karen will want to open a second bottle before dessert. Tell Daniel Tyler would love to kick a ball around before it gets dark.

See you at five. Thank you.
snippet:
Lamb shoulder. You are a good woman, Mum.

I've got Rosa covering my Monday morn...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house - you and the kids?

body: Randy, I've been thinking about getting everyone together this Sunday — Karen and Daniel are free and it's been too long. I'd love to see the grandchildren if Megan and Tyler can make it. Nothing elaborate, just the usual. Let me know if your schedule is going to cooperate for once. You know how that court calendar of yours likes to have other plans. — Mom
snippet: Randy, I've been thinking about getting everyone together this Sunday — Karen an...
subject: Sunday dinner at the house

body: I'll check with Megan and Tyler this afternoon — Meg has been hard to pin down lately but I think she's free. Tyler's always easier. My calendar looks clear after noon on Sunday, though I've got a pretrial Friday that could bleed into the weekend if things go sideways. Count me in provisionally, firming up by Thursday. What time are you thinking? — Randy
snippet: I'll check with Megan and Tyler this afternoon — Meg has been hard to pin down l...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house

body: Let's say 2 o'clock — that gives you time for your run first. I know you won't skip Sabino even on a Sunday, so I'm not going to pretend you will. Karen is bringing enchiladas and Daniel is bringing whatever Daniel considers a contribution. I'll handle everything else. Just tell me if Megan has any new dietary requirements going on — she seems to change her mind every other month and I don't want a repeat of Easter. — Mom
snippet: Let's say 2 o'clock — that gives you time for your run first. I know you won't s...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house

body: 2 works perfectly. Megan is currently eating everything, which is honestly a relief after the phase where nothing was allowed to have had a face. Tyler will eat three of Karen's enchiladas and look innocent about it. I'll bring something from the bakery on Speedway — the one you liked last time. Pretrial wrapped this morning so Sunday is properly clear now, no caveats. Confirmed. — Randy
snippet: 2 works perfectly. Megan is currently eating everything, which is honestly a rel...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house

body: Wonderful. See everyone at 2. And Randy — if you're passing anywhere near Desert Roasters on the way over, bring the good coffee. I've been making do with the grocery store stuff for two weeks and it shows in my mood. Ask Karen, she'll tell you. Sunday. Don't be late. — Mom
snippet: Wonderful. See everyone at 2. And Randy — if you're passing anywhere near Desert...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house

body: Randy, you said last Sunday the oil on the RAV4 is well overdue and you haven't had a minute to sort it. I called Midas on Grant Road this morning and they've got an 8am slot Wednesday. If you leave the car at ours Tuesday evening Daniel can follow me over and bring me back. They only hold slots until noon so just let me know before then and I'll book it.
snippet: Randy, you said last Sunday the oil on the RAV4 is well overdue and you hav...
subject: Your car service - I can take it in Wednesday morning

body:
Perfect. I'll drop it Tuesday after the Sabino run - should be at yours by seven. Keys in the cupholder.

One thing: the front tyre pressure light has been on and off for two weeks. Worth flagging when you drop it off so they check the spare too. And yes, I know. Two weeks is too long. Court's been a lot.

Thank you.
snippet: Perfect. I'll drop it Tuesday after the Sabino run - should be at yours by seven...
subject: Re: Your car service - I can take it in Wednesday morning

body:
Randy,

The desert willow out back finally did something worth seeing and it got me thinking we haven't walked Tohono Chul together in ages. Are you free this Sunday? I'd say eight-thirty so we beat the heat — we can do the full loop and I'll make huevos rancheros after if you want to come back to the house.

Karen and Daniel might join for the food part but it'll just be us for the walk. Let me know what your morning looks like.

Mom
snippet:
Randy,

The desert willow out back finally did something worth seeing and it got...
subject: Sunday morning at Tohono Chul — you free?

body:
Eight-thirty works. I'll do the Sabino loop early — out by six, back by seven-thirty — so I'll have time to shower and drive over. Bring sunscreen; I know you won't but I'm saying it anyway.

If Karen and Daniel are coming for brunch, tell Daniel to bring the salsa verde he made last time. That's become my favourite thing he's ever cooked. The jar he sent home with me lasted four days and I was rationing it.

Randy
snippet: Eight-thirty works. I'll do the Sabino loop early — out by six, back by seven-th...
subject: Re: Sunday morning at Tohono Chul — you free?

body:
I already have sunscreen, I'm not entirely hopeless. I'll pass the message to Daniel — he'll be pleased, he's been trying to get that recipe from his aunt for thirty years.

Eight-thirty at the main entrance then. I'll have coffee ready at the house after. I found a bag of that Dark Sky blend you like from Desert Roasters, so no excuses for leaving early.

Mom
snippet: I already have sunscreen, I'm not entirely hopeless. I'll pass the message to Da...
subject: Re: Sunday morning at Tohono Chul — you free?
